LandingPage.title: LandingPage
LandingPage.description: Represents a single landing page designed to receive traffic and display dynamic advertisements. This is the page users from traffic sources like Facebook will see.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LandingPage entity.

name.description: A human-readable name for the landing page (e.g., 'Facebook Arbitrage Page').

slug.description: A URL-friendly slug that uniquely identifies the landing page for external access (e.g., 'my-fb-offer-1').

description.description: A brief description of the landing page's purpose or content.

htmlContent.description: The full HTML content or a template string for the landing page, where ad scripts will be dynamically injected.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the landing page was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the landing page was last updated.

AdCampaign.title: AdCampaign
AdCampaign.description: Groups together multiple ad slot configurations for a specific advertising initiative on a landing page.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdCampaign entity.

name.description: A human-readable name for the ad campaign (e.g., 'Direct Link Summer Promo').

description.description: A brief description of the ad campaign's goals or content.

landingPageId.description: Reference to the LandingPage where this campaign is deployed. (Relationship: LandingPage 1:N AdCampaign)

isActive.description: A boolean flag indicating if the ad campaign is currently active and serving ads.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the ad campaign was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the ad campaign was last updated.

AdSlotConfiguration.title: AdSlotConfiguration
AdSlotConfiguration.description: Defines a specific advertisement type and its content, along with targeting rules based on browser and adblocker status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdSlotConfiguration entity.

campaignId.description: Reference to the AdCampaign this ad slot belongs to. (Relationship: AdCampaign 1:N AdSlotConfiguration)

name.description: A descriptive name for this specific ad slot configuration (e.g., 'Direct Link for Chrome, No Adblocker').

adType.description: The type of advertisement format used from Adsterra.

adContent.description: The actual Adsterra script, direct link URL, or other content for this ad slot.

targetBrowser.description: The browser type this ad configuration is primarily intended for. 'All' implies no specific browser targeting.

targetAdblockerStatus.description: The adblocker detection status this ad configuration is intended for. 'All' implies no specific adblocker status targeting.

priority.description: A numerical value indicating the preference for this ad slot when multiple configurations match; lower numbers indicate higher priority for selection.

isActive.description: A boolean flag indicating if this specific ad slot configuration is active and available for serving.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the ad slot configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the ad slot configuration was last updated.

AdImpressionEvent.title: AdImpressionEvent
AdImpressionEvent.description: Records an instance of an advertisement being successfully displayed to a user on a landing page.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdImpressionEvent entity.

adSlotConfigurationId.description: Reference to the AdSlotConfiguration that was served. (Relationship: AdSlotConfiguration 1:N AdImpressionEvent)

landingPageId.description: Reference to the LandingPage where the impression occurred. (Relationship: LandingPage 1:N AdImpressionEvent)

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the ad impression occurred.

browserType.description: The type of browser detected at the time of the impression (e.g., 'Chrome', 'Firefox').

adblockerStatus.description: The status of adblocker detection at the time of the impression ('detected', 'notDetected', 'unknown').

AdClickEvent.title: AdClickEvent
AdClickEvent.description: Records an instance of a user clicking on an advertisement displayed on a landing page.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdClickEvent entity.

adSlotConfigurationId.description: Reference to the AdSlotConfiguration that was clicked. (Relationship: AdSlotConfiguration 1:N AdClickEvent)

landingPageId.description: Reference to the LandingPage where the click occurred. (Relationship: LandingPage 1:N AdClickEvent)

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the ad click occurred.

browserType.description: The type of browser detected at the time of the click.

adblockerStatus.description: The status of adblocker detection at the time of the click.

definition.description: Top-level collection for owner-specific administrative data. This path identifies the owner for all nested private documents, providing the primary authorization context.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the authenticated owner/admin.

definition.description: Stores all administrative details of a landing page, managed by the owner. This is the source of truth for design and settings. Authorization is strictly tied to the parent 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the owner.

params.name: landingPageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific LandingPage.

definition.description: Stores details for an ad campaign associated with a specific landing page, managed by the owner. Authorization is inherited from the parent 'userId' and 'landingPageId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the owner.

params.name: landingPageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent LandingPage.

params.name: campaignId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AdCampaign.

definition.description: Stores detailed ad slot configurations for a specific campaign, managed by the owner. Authorization is inherited from the parent 'userId', 'landingPageId', and 'campaignId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the owner.

params.name: landingPageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent LandingPage.

params.name: campaignId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent AdCampaign.

params.name: adSlotConfigurationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AdSlotConfiguration.

definition.description: Records ad impressions. This data is collected from public landing pages but is accessible only by the owner for analytics. Includes denormalized 'landingPageId' and 'adSlotConfigurationId' for query flexibility.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the owner.

params.name: impressionId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AdImpressionEvent.

definition.description: Records ad clicks. This data is collected from public landing pages but is accessible only by the owner for analytics. Includes denormalized 'landingPageId' and 'adSlotConfigurationId' for query flexibility.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the owner.

params.name: clickId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AdClickEvent.

definition.description: Stores publicly accessible HTML content and active campaign references for client-side rendering. This collection is populated and updated by a Cloud Function. It includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'activeCampaignId' to avoid authorization dependencies. Public read access allowed for client-side rendering.

params.name: landingPageSlug
params.description: The URL-friendly slug that uniquely identifies the landing page for public access.

definition.description: Stores active and publicly accessible ad slot configuration details (ad script, targeting rules) for client-side ad serving logic. This collection is populated and updated by a Cloud Function. It includes denormalized 'ownerId', 'landingPageId', and 'campaignId' for client-side queryability without authorization dependencies. Public read access allowed for client-side ad serving logic.

params.name: adSlotConfigurationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AdSlotConfiguration, used for public access.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Ceasarion is designed with a strict segregation of administrative (owner-managed) data and publicly accessible data. This ensures robust security, clear authorization intent, and adherence to Query-able Authorization Predicates (QAPs).

**Owner-Managed Data (Private):**
All core management entities (Landing Pages, Ad Campaigns, Ad Slot Configurations, and Analytics) are nested directly under the `/users/{userId}` path. This establishes a strong, path-based ownership model where `request.auth.uid` must match the `{userId}` wildcard in the path for any read or write operations. This inherently provides Authorization Independence because a document's authorization context (its owner) is explicitly part of its path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents to check ownership. For example, to access an Ad Campaign, the rule `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;` is sufficient and atomic. Analytics events are also nested under the user's path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/adImpressionEvents/{impressionId}`) to ensure only the owner can view their aggregate statistics, while still allowing efficient collection-level queries with `landingPageId` and `adSlotConfigurationId` fields.

**Publicly Accessible Data (Client-Facing):**
To achieve QAPs for public access and strict Authorization Independence, public-facing data is stored in dedicated, top-level collections: `/public_landing_pages/{landingPageSlug}` and `/public_ad_configurations/{adSlotConfigurationId}`. These collections contain a carefully curated subset of data necessary for client-side rendering and ad serving, such as `htmlContent` and `adContent`, along with essential identifiers like `activeCampaignId`, `campaignId`, and `landingPageId` (all denormalized). These public collections are configured with `allow read: if true;` rules, making them globally readable without authentication. The crucial aspect here is that these public documents are populated and updated by a secure Cloud Function that listens for changes in the owner's private `/users/{userId}/...` data. This design completely decouples public read access from private management, ensuring that public requests never traverse the private user hierarchy or make authorization-dependent `get()` calls in security rules. This radical segregation provides clear QAPs for both public and private data: private data is queried by owner ID, and public data is queried without any authorization predicates, directly fetching what is needed.

**Denormalization for Authorization Independence:**
*   For owner-managed data, authorization is strictly path-based, leveraging the `{userId}` wildcard. Subcollection documents implicitly inherit the owner's context from their parent path, making them authorization-independent at their own level.
*   For public data, critical fields like `activeCampaignId`, `campaignId`, `landingPageId`, and `ownerId` are denormalized directly into the `/public_landing_pages/{landingPageSlug}` and `/public_ad_configurations/{adSlotConfigurationId}` documents. This means clients requesting public content have all the necessary information (e.g., which campaign is active for a landing page, or which ad slot configs belong to a campaign) available within the document or via direct query on the public collection. No `get()` operations are needed to check access to a 'parent' private document, as the public documents are self-contained for their access purposes.
